mnemonics: Stack Deep Dive: DFS goes DEEP first, using a STACK (LIFO) to remember where to backtrack.
keyPoints: Explores a graph/tree by going as deep as possible down one branch before backtracking.
Implemented using a stack data structure (either explicitly or via recursion).
Not guaranteed to find the shortest path in an unweighted graph (unlike BFS).
commonMistakes: Forgetting to mark nodes as visited, which can lead to infinite loops in graphs with cycles.
Confusing its properties with Breadth-First Search (BFS), especially regarding completeness and optimality.
Assuming it will find the optimal solution, when it may only find a solution (not necessarily the best one).
